name: Brandied Quail
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut quail into serving pieces using breasts only or breasts and legs if desired.
2. Brown slowly in half of the butter for about 30 minutes until nicely browned on all sides.
3. Push the quail to one side and add currant or apricot jelly to the drippings.
4. Add the remaining butter, a dash of salt, and stir well as the jelly melts.
5. When the sauce is well blended, spread the browned pieces of quail back on the pan and baste with the sauce.
6. Cover and let simmer until the meat is fork tender.
7. Skim off any excess fat and add brandy to the mixture according to taste preference.
8. Stir in the brandy just until heated and serve.
Serves 2 to 4
Source: "Mountain Measures"--Junior League of Charleston, WV ed. 1974
